data.title: Extended Downturn Triggers Fetching Chewy Stock (CHWY) Options Trade
data.description:
Chewy (CHWY) might not be the first idea that comes to mind for upside opportunities, but a possible resurgence could help shift the narrative. Over the past five years, CHWY stock has lost nearly 68% of its value, a victim of the inability to replicate the enthusiasm of the unique COVID-impacted market paradigm. Combined with broader economic challenges of the new normal, investors began to de-risk their exposure to the pet e-commerce specialist.

data.title: "Pro-Consumer, Pro-Worker" Netflix Stock (NASDAQ:NFLX) Slips as Warner Deal Backlash Mounts
data.description:
Streaming giant Netflix (NFLX) is starting to take a lot of fire over the acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), and the reasons behind that fire are oddly rational. The fire itself, meanwhile, is making for some very concerned investors, and shares of Netflix reflected that concern as they dropped nearly 2.5% in the closing minutes of Wednesday's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The first major problem is that the plan to buy Warner violates a key bit of Netflix historical philosophy. For years, Netflix has labored under the notion of "build, not buy." Now, Netflix is visibly turning its back on the principle that got it to this point in the first place. That is a problem for many investors, but the list of issues goes on from there.

Netflix declared the move to buy Warner was both "pro-consumer" and "pro-worker," pointing out that both businesses would need new teams and mean more opportunities for creatives. This also fell flat, as investors likely wondered how losing one potential customer, in a market that did not exactly have many to begin with, would mean more opportunities for creatives to sell their films and series. The deal has not even made contact with regulators yet, and there are deep concerns about whether it will pass muster.

The App Is Getting Changed Too

Meanwhile, Netflix is starting to realize that it will be competing with several other apps for attention going forward, including TikTok. And Netflix realized that its means of presenting video could work out quite similarly, thus, it is bringing out vertical video presentation in its mobile app.

It will be similar to the changes it made to the TV user interface not so long ago, reports note. Though it will take a while before these changes actually arrive. Netflix plans to bring out the new app "…later in 2026." The move also reflects Netflix's recent move into podcasts, reports note, so this move to add vertical video is really a refinement over things Netflix has already done.

Is Netflix Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on NFLX stock based on 26 Buys, 11 Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 8.53% loss in its share price over the past year, the average NFLX price target of $117.06 per share implies 37.04% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Meta Will Begin Showing Ads on Threads to Users Worldwide
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META), the parent company of Facebook and Instagram, said that it will begin showing ads on its Threads platform to users around the world starting next week. In a blog post, Meta said that ads will allow businesses to join conversations on Threads while reaching people who are most interested in their products. The rollout will happen gradually and could take several months, but it is an important step in turning Threads into a money-making platform.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

It's worth noting that Meta previously tested ads on Threads with users in the U.S. and Japan last January. Importantly, Wall Street analysts expect Threads to become a meaningful new source of revenue for Meta. In addition, Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg said in August that Threads had already grown to more than 400 million monthly active users, which shows just how quickly the platform has gained traction.

More recently, reports noted that Threads has surpassed X in daily active mobile users worldwide, citing data from Similarweb (SMWB). Looking ahead, Meta said it plans to continue adding new features to Threads, including additional ad formats and third-party verification tools that already exist on Facebook and Instagram.

What Is the Price Target for Meta?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on META stock based on 37 Buys, six Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average META price target of $821.33 per share implies 33% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: SanDisk Stock Soars to Record High Ahead of Q2 Earnings – Buy Now or Wait?
data.description:
SanDisk stock (SNDK) hit a new all-time high, gaining 2.4% in early trading today. In the previous session, SNDK stock gained 9.5%, topping the S&P 500 (SPX). The latest surge came after five-star-rated analyst Asiya Merchant at Citi raised her price target on SNDK from $280 to $490, maintaining a Buy rating. Even after the stock's sharp run-up, SanDisk remains on Citi's short-term upside watch list ahead of its Q2 earnings report on January 29. Merchant also expects the tight supply and strong demand environment that has driven the stock higher to remain in place through 2027.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That said, Citi noted that other memory and storage stocks could offer better upside, given how sharply SanDisk shares have already climbed over the past year. The firm currently prefers hard disk drive makers such as Western Digital (WDC) and Seagate Technology (STX), which may have more room to run as drive prices increase.

In Q2, Wall Street expects the company to post earnings of $3.32 per share, up sharply from $1.23 a year ago. Meanwhile, revenue is forecast to reach $2.63 billion.

Why Did Citi Raise SanDisk's Price Target to $490?

SNDK's price target upgrade came as part of Citi's 2026 outlook for the technology hardware sector. Merchant said hyperscaler data center spending remains strong, supporting demand across key areas like power, storage, connectors, and fiber. That spending backdrop is good news for companies like SanDisk, which directly benefit from growing demand for data center and enterprise storage. Merchant said the company is still seeing strong, unconstrained demand, with growth running in the mid-20% range.

In addition, Merchant said SanDisk is not changing its supply plans, choosing instead to balance near-term profits with long-term growth goals in an uncertain market.

Is SanDisk a Buy or Sell?

So far this year, SNDK is up about 90%. Even more eye-catching, the stock has surged over 1,100% since spinning off from Western Digital last February, making it one of the market's biggest breakout stories.

Looking ahead, Wall Street analysts rate SanDisk stock as a Moderate Buy, with 11 Buy ratings and four Holds over the past three months. However, the recent rally has pushed the stock well above its average price target of $357.53, suggesting more than 20% downside from current levels.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Trump Cancels Greenland Tariffs on Eight European Countries
data.description:
President Trump announced on Wednesday that he will not move forward with his original proposal to impose a 10% tariff on eight European countries. The decision followed talks with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte that resulted in a framework to acquire the self-governing territory.

data.title: Restaurant Steak n Shake to Pay Workers a Bitcoin (BTC) Bonus
data.description:
U.S. restaurant chain Steak n Shake is offering its hourly employees a new Bitcoin (BTC) bonus.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

On social media, the hamburger chain, which is owned by Biglari Holdings (BH), said it will pay its workers a Bitcoin bonus worth $0.21 for every hour they work. The bonus applies to all staff at the company's locations starting on March 1 of this year.

However, the accumulated Bitcoin rewards won't vest for two years. The crypto bonus is Steak n Shake's latest embrace of Bitcoin, which it started accepting as payment at all of its U.S. locations last year. Earlier in January, Steak n Shake added $10 million worth of Bitcoin to its corporate treasury.

Attracting Younger Diners

Management at Steak n Shake have said that accepting BTC as payment has helped to reduce its card processing fees while also attracting a younger demographic to its restaurants. Same-store sales at Steak n Shake rose by more than 10% in the second quarter of 2025 after the Bitcoin payment was announced.

Steak n Shake is doing everything it can to market and promote its Bitcoin association. The company has even launched a Bitcoin-themed burger that includes a small donation to open-source Bitcoin development.

Is Bitcoin a Buy?

Most analysts don't offer ratings or price targets on Bitcoin. So instead, we'll look at the three-month performance of BTC. As one can see in the chart below, the price of Bitcoin has fallen 16.99% in the last 12 weeks.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Pending Home Sales Plunge as Trump Signs Order to Block Institutional Purchases
data.description:
U.S. pending home sales, which track signed contracts for existing homes, fell by 9.3% month-over-month in December amid elevated mortgage rates, low inventory, and high median home prices. As of January 15, the average rate on a 30-year mortgage was 6.06%, according to Freddie Mac (FMCC).

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"Consumers prefer seeing abundant inventory before making the major decision of purchasing a home," said National Association of Realtors Chief Economist Lawrence Yun. "So, the decline in pending home sales could be a result of dampened consumer enthusiasm about buying a home when there are so few options listed for sale."

Trump Moves to Curb Institutional Home Purchases

President Trump believes that housing inventory could increase with fewer institutional homebuyers. On Tuesday, he signed an executive order banning large institutional investors from purchasing single-family homes.

As part of the order, the Secretary of the Treasury will define the terms "large institutional investor" and "single-family homes" within 30 days. Within 60 days, federal agencies will provide guidance to implement the order. The White House added that the new policy will prioritize families over big Wall Street players and that "People live in homes, not corporations."

data.title: Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.B) Poised to Take $2.5 Billion Loss on Kraft Heinz Stake
data.description:
Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.B) looks poised to sell its 27% stake in food giant Kraft Heinz (KHC) for a $2.5 billion loss.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Berkshire currently owns 325.4 million shares of KHC stock worth about $7.3 billion based on the market price. However, Berkshire's cost basis to acquire the Kraft Heinz shares is $9.8 billion, meaning the holding company is likely to incur a $2.5 billion loss if it sells the entire position.

Yet Berkshire Hathaway has filed a prospectus with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) that seems to indicate the company plans to offload its entire stake in Kraft Heinz. This seems to be one of the first moves being undertaken by new Berkshire CEO Greg Abel, who took over from Warren Buffett at the start of this year.

Warren Buffett's Golden Rule

The sale of the Kraft Heinz stake appears to violate Warren Buffett's golden rule of investing, which is "don't lose money." His second investing rule is "don't forget rule number one." Yet Buffett himself has famously criticized the investment in Kraft Heinz, which has failed to deliver a meaningful return.

Buffett famously helped to orchestrate a merger of Kraft and Heinz in 2015, and then purchased 27% of the combined company's stock. Initially the merger of the two food giants was praised. But KHC stock has since fallen 75% from its post-merger peak of more than $90 a share in 2017.

Buffett has frequently cited the Kraft Heinz investment as one of his biggest blunders. New CEO Abel appears to be trying to correct that mistake, even if it means taking a sizable loss for Berkshire Hathaway.

Is BRK.B Stock a Buy?

Only a couple of analysts currently offer a rating and price target on Berkshire Hathaway's more affordable Class B stock. So instead, we'll look at the shares' three-month performance. As one can see in the chart below, shares of BRK.B have declined 1.31% over the past 12 weeks.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Trump's Bid to Fire Fed's Lisa Cook Faces Supreme Court Scrutiny
data.description:
The Supreme Court heard oral arguments on Tuesday in the case regarding President Trump's attempt to fire Fed Governor Lisa Cook. Last year, Federal Housing Finance Agency (FHFA) Director Bill Pulte brought to Trump's attention that Cook allegedly committed mortgage fraud by listing two separate homes as her primary residence in order to take advantage of tax benefits. Cook denied the allegations.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

While SCOTUS justices expressed skepticism over allowing Trump to terminate Cook, they were less certain about how to resolve the case.

Justices Split Over Scope of Trump-Cook Case

The justices are split on issuing a broad or narrow ruling. A narrow ruling could suggest that Trump didn't provide Cook with adequate notice of her dismissal or an opportunity to address the accusations. A broad ruling would hinge on federal law, which states that Fed officials can only be fired "for cause." Another option on the table is for SCOTUS to send the case back to a lower court without providing a ruling.

The case will either be "guided by evidence and independent judgment or will succumb to political pressure," said Cook after the hearing.

Regardless of the outcome, the ruling will set a legal precedent for the independence of the Fed and the limits of presidential authority over its members.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: $7,150 Price Target Emerges for Gold as Metal Hits New High
data.description:
Gold (GLD) prices crossed above the $4,800 level on Wednesday as investors looked for safety amid fresh tariff threats and fears of a global trade war. After a historic run in 2025, gold has entered 2026 with strong momentum, supported by geopolitical tensions, lower real interest rates, and a growing move away from the U.S. dollar. As a result, some forecasts are becoming especially aggressive, with ICBC Standard Bank strategist Julia Du saying that gold could climb as high as $7,150 if these trends remain in place.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Moreover, overall market expectations are turning increasingly bullish. In fact, analysts surveyed by the London Bullion Market Association (LBMA) see gold rising above $5,000 this year, driven by Federal Reserve easing expectations, falling U.S. real rates, and ongoing central bank buying. Unsurprisingly, the LBMA described gold as the standout asset following its record-breaking performance last year, which helps reinforce the view that current conditions remain highly supportive.

In addition, Goldman Sachs (GS) has called gold its highest-conviction trade, driven by a shift in demand from central banks to private investors, such as wealth managers, hedge funds, and pension funds. Importantly, analysts argue that this rally does not look like a speculative bubble but rather part of a longer-term trend that has been marked by ongoing geopolitical tensions and countries' increasing focus on securing critical resources.

Is Gold a Good Buy?

Using TipRanks' technical analysis tool, the indicators seem to point to a positive outlook for gold. Indeed, the summary section pictured below shows that 14 indicators are Bullish, compared to two Neutral and six Bearish indicators.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri
